prompt:
The owner reviewed this PR and found it too focused on his personal setup: the-valley is a general open-source project, and its durable knowledge (decisions/ideas) must stay portable; deployment-instance detail belongs to the consumer's infrastructure repo. Rework the decision node accordingly.

Read .the-valley/README.md for the node convention first.

1. Replace .the-valley/decisions/dcr-db1acbb-hetzner-replication-mechanism.md with .the-valley/decisions/dcr-98508bf-replication-mechanism.md (slug 'replication-mechanism'; id 98508bf is sha256-derived — first 7 hex of sha256 of the slug). Frontmatter otherwise per convention (type decision, status decided, created 2026-07-04).
2. Rewrite the body in portable mechanism classes, no vendor or host names in the decision itself: the durability mechanism for a valley's primary store is (a) a push-triggered git-native mirror to an independent live remote — RPO is approximately zero because 'pushed = replicated within minutes' is the S1 bar, and the mirror is directly fetchable — plus (b) periodic encrypted, deduplicated backup to cheap offsite storage for history depth. Block-level filesystem replication (ZFS-send-style) was considered and rejected: it requires a filesystem-capable receive target and the far copy is not a usable git remote. Note that per-project push mirrors (the schema's mirrors field) are the implementation surface for (a).
3. End with a short 'Pilot binding' paragraph: one or two sentences noting the pilot instantiates (a) on a small Hetzner VPS and (b) on a Hetzner Storage Box, with the primary on the owner's tailnet host; instance configuration lives in the consumer repo (cosmo). This is the only place instance names appear.
4. Update the status-flip edit to .the-valley/outcomes/oc-fc348f0-hetzner-mechanism.md so its added line links to the NEW node id/filename. Do not rename oc-fc348f0 itself — outcomes are situated work-tracking and may name pilot infra; only the decision node must be portable.
5. Verify relative links resolve. Update the PR title/description to reflect the rework (portable decision + pilot binding).

Push to this PR's branch; do not merge.
